title: Mirror Distance of an Integer
content:
You are given an integer n.

Define its mirror distance as: abs(n - reverse(n))‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč where reverse(n) is the integer formed by reversing the digits of n.

Return an integer denoting the mirror distance of n‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč.

abs(x) denotes the absolute value of x.

Example 1:

Input: n = 25

Output: 27

Explanation:

‚ÄĘ reverse(25) = 52.

‚ÄĘ Thus, the answer is abs(25 - 52) = 27.

Example 2:

Input: n = 10

Output: 9

Explanation:

‚ÄĘ reverse(10) = 01 which is 1.

‚ÄĘ Thus, the answer is abs(10 - 1) = 9.

Example 3:

Input: n = 7

Output: 0

Explanation:

‚ÄĘ reverse(7) = 7.

‚ÄĘ Thus, the answer is abs(7 - 7) = 0.

Constraints:

‚ÄĘ 1 <= n <= 109

solutions.explanation: Reverse the digits of n to get its mirror; return the absolute difference. O(d) time where d is the digit count.
